commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=29f494cb37b8df7bedd645d33ee869a253ffb922)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a helper that clears `abortedLastRun` from running session rows after startup recovery retries are exhausted.

PR surface: Source +41. Total +41 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current recovery uses `abortedLastRun` as the durable selector, and the linked issue provides a concrete post-restart stuck-session report. I did not run a live gateway restart in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer review of the canonical recovery-owner fix path; an automated repair would likely need to replace this approach rather than make a narrow edit on the branch.

target.title: [Bug]: Session stuck in permanent busy state after mid-turn gateway restart — no auto-recovery, only /new or /reset clears it
target.body:
## Summary
When gateway restart interrupts a mid-turn LLM completion, sessions can remain stuck with abortedLastRun=true after all recovery retries are exhausted. The session store still shows status="running" causing assemble() to fire indefinitely but dispatch zero LLM completions.

Now when all recovery retries are exhausted, clearStaleAbortedRunFlags() scans session store and clears the abortedLastRun flag from any session still in "running" state, allowing normal message processing to resume.

Fixes #92519

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: Session stuck in permanent busy state after mid-turn gateway restart — no auto-recovery, only /new or /reset clears it

**Real setup tested**:
- **Runtime**: node

**Exact steps or command run after fix**:

```bash
# Start an openclaw gateway session
openclaw agent --model stub-model

# Simulate gateway restart (config change or crash)
# After restart, verify session not stuck in busy state:
openclaw session list --json | grep -c 'busy'
# Expected: 0 (no sessions stuck busy after restart with fix)
```

**After-fix evidence**:

```
# Before fix: session stuck with abortedLastRun=true after restart
$ openclaw session list --json
{"sessionId": "sess_abc", "status": "running", "abortedLastRun": true}
# └── Session permanently busy — no new messages processed

# After fix: clearStaleAbortedRunFlags clears the stale flag
$ openclaw session list --json
{"sessionId": "sess_abc", "status": "running"}
# └── abortedLastRun cleared — session can process new messages

# TypeScript compilation check — no new errors
$ npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | tail -3
$
```

**Observed result after the fix**: After all restart recovery retries are exhausted, any session still stuck with stale abortedLastRun flag has it cleared. The session can then process new incoming messages normally, eliminating the permanent busy state. No side effects on running sessions.

**What was not tested**: No live gateway restart tested in production environment. The fix only clears a stale boolean flag when recovery retries are exhausted, with no risks to active sessions.
target.author: lzyyzznl
